description: Shipping barge crew reported a submerged blue light pacing them for 12 minutes. No wake or surface object was visible.

location_name: Western Puerto Rico (rural, son of landowner Feliberti)
description: 9-year-old Juan Rivera Feliberti testing a kite; kite became stuck to large round sparking aerial object ('It gave off a lot of sparks and was very round'). An unknown force drew him into the object. Inside: a slightly shorter girl with bronzed white skin, and a tall blond human-looking man. Girl showed him colored-light toys and produced simian imp creatures from a box via spiral smoke. Young Feliberti traded his kite for the box. The imps produced from the box became an ongoing high-strangeness phenomenon in his adult life (see Salvador Freixedo's Ellos). First documented PR contact case. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales/Magdalena del Amo-Freixedo 2012.
A Pan American Airways Sikorsky S‑38 amphibious plane struck an unidentified submerged object while landing in Ponce harbor. (No casualties were reported) .
Naval cadet observed a silver “needle” zipping silently across the southern sky during a daylight exercise.
British destroyer HMS Vidette observed a luminous submerged object accelerating eastward, briefly tracked by hydrophone.

US Navy Armed Guard aboard the freighter SS Cornelia reported an unidentified submerged object (USO) in the water near San Juan. The watchstander noted the object left a wake and was unable to identify it. He notified his superiors, a destroyer was dispatched, and by the next morning the destroyer had engaged and sunk a German U‑boat at that location .

Splitting of a pilot balloon image into two or more portions, initially attributed to double refraction at a high atmospheric layer. Report suggests balloon may have actually burst into two hemispheres, each maintaining its shape before breaking into smaller solid pieces.

The U.S. Coast Guard cutter Spencer (WPG-36) was patrolling near Puerto Rico when she detected an underwater contact at about 1,200 yards. The crew dropped multiple depth charges in successive salvos, and eventually noted a large oil slick on the surface where the contact had been. (No wreckage or submarine was found, suggesting an unresolved underwater anomaly)
Two U.S. Army airmen observed a perfectly circular craft hovering silently, then suddenly accelerated away at tremendous speed.

Morning. Young Luis Villafañe saw a small creature (~3 ft tall) approach the door of his house wearing coveralls of brownish hue, with large black eyes and long fingers reaching to knees. When he alerted his mother and siblings, all were seized by sudden paralysis. The being regarded them for what seemed a long time, then dashed off at high speed into dense tropical vegetation. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Earth's Shadow Denizens (2012).
Mrs. Maria Ayuso and husband saw a bright, fast-moving reflective “disk‑like” object at 17:30.
Americo Paoli and three others saw four luminous discs flying in pairs. Described as shiny with a straw-like color; observed from outside a pharmacy.
El Mundo reporter Miguel Angel Santin and colleague saw a luminous object with a greenish cast, flying east to west at low azimuth.

location_name: Atlantic Fleet Weapons Training Facility, Vieques
description: U.S. Navy personnel observed maneuvering, hovering objects near naval exercises off Puerto Rico’s coast.

location_name: Santa Rita urbanization, San Juan, Puerto Rico
description: Circular yellowish orb of light first mistaken for a balloon. Repeated orbits around the Santa Rita urbanization. Apparent size of a dime. Multiple named witnesses on the same street. Source: Sebastián Robiou Lamarche, Manifiesto OVNI; cited by Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010).

Pan American Flight 203 (DC-4), Capt. Cent/1st Ofcr Gallagher at 8000 ft saw brilliant white object approach from left under overcast at 10000 ft and streak by left wing ¼ mile, followed by 2 smaller orange balls of fire.

location_name: Mayagüez, Puerto Rico (view over city and bay)
description: 7:00 PM. Prominent politician Miguel Ángel García, wife, daughter, and son-in-law observed two orange disks — one larger than the other — flying high over the Mona Passage. Larger disk appeared sun-sized and static; smaller maneuvered around it, switching positions. García used binoculars. Daughter Fredita attempted to photograph the aerial display but nothing appeared on Verichrome film. Other Mayagüez residents saw the disks, attributed them to Ramey AFB tests. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010); Robiou Lamarche.

7 p.m. Attorney/ex-USAF pilot Mr. Stipes and Sr. García-Méndez saw 2 shining orange spheres: one stationary, the other darted away and back for 30 minutes. No sound or exhaust. (Blue Book Unknown BBU 1213)

Mr. R.B. Stipes (ex-USAF pilot) and Mr. Miguel García-Méndez (attorney), along with their wives, witnessed two bright orange spheres in the sky over Mayagüez. One sphere remained stationary while the other darted away and back repeatedly for ~30 minutes. No sound or exhaust was heard .
Hotel guests and staff at Caribe Hilton Hotel (Tutela & Rodriguez) observed two saucer‑shaped objects over the ocean at 19:45 heading NNE.
Robert Daly reported seeing two discs over San Juan at 04:30 AM.
USS Wasp radar tracked a fast-moving object at 1,200 mph and 50,000 ft. A DF‑9 fighter attempted intercept but failed to close distance.

In fall 1952, three Pan American Airways pilots (Captains Zammett, Harris, Hutchins) on a flight from New York toward San Juan observed a large metallic UFO. The object hovered nearby for several minutes, then accelerated away at high speed .

Merchant Jaime Báez and schoolteacher Aida Reyes among witnesses: a flying saucer described as 'considerable size and unexpected brightness' hovered over a sugar refinery. Reyes had to close her eyes due to brilliance comparable to the sun. Object oval-shaped and moving silently. Part of early 1950s Puerto Rico saucer wave. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010); Adjuntas article (2015).

location_name: Punta Guanajibo area, near Cabo Rojo lighthouse, Mayagüez, Puerto Rico
description: Early evening. Photographer Frank McFerran and wife driving near Mayagüez observed brilliant light changing colors and casting reflections on sea surface. They parked at Punta Guanajibo to observe the object near the Cabo Rojo lighthouse. After 20 minutes of color changes, the object vanished; witnesses speculated it plunged into the ocean. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010).
Photographer Frank McFerran and wife saw a brilliant color‑changing light reflecting off the sea at Punta Guanajibo (~20 min).

7:30 p.m. Schoolteacher Aida Reyes observed oval-shaped object of considerable size hovering over a sugar refinery. Light was so intense she had to close her eyes. Object was larger than a barrel lid, moved silently across sky. Source: Sebastián Robiou, Manifiesto OVNI.

A sugar refinery schoolteacher, Aida Reyes, witnessed an oval “flying saucer” hovering silently above a sugar refinery. The object’s brightness was so intense that looking at it felt like viewing the Sun through closed eyelids.
Adjuntas Mayor and others saw three disc‑shaped lights in the sky changing intensity and color as they quickly moved overhead.

location_name: Floral Park, Hato Rey, Puerto Rico
description: 7:00-8:00 PM. Mr. Buenaventura Quiñones and wife Sylvia observed a glowing disk making a buzzing sound similar to an electric motor. Neighbors had seen the same object on previous evenings at the same time of night. Object flew so low that observers heard what they took to be its engine, causing confusion. Part of 1952 Puerto Rico saucer wave. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010); Robiou Lamarche.
Mr. Buenaventura Quiñones and his wife saw a glowing disk at ~7–8 PM so low they heard a buzzing motor sound; neighbors had seen it on previous nights too.

Crew of a USAF RB-36 bomber (Capt. Gennrich, Lt. Moore, A1C Eakovich) observed a spherical red-orange light (~8 ft diameter) approach and pass just above the left wing (~300 ft away) of their aircraft. The object then suddenly shot upward and disappeared out of sight .

Five witnesses observed an unidentified flying object. Witness group reportedly included three U.S. Army captains, one sergeant, and one civilian. Object was described as a bright star or bright ball of fire at great height, moving rapidly. Report was received from Major Bradford P. Shuman, Wing Intelligence Officer, Ramey Air Force Base. Two RB-36 aircraft were reportedly operating in the area at the time, at approximately 12,000 and 21,000 feet.
source_citation: https://www.war.gov/medialink/ufo/release_1/65_hs1-834228961_62-hq-83894_section_7.pdf; 62-hq-83894_section_7.pdf p.142; Ramey AFB IR-22-53 / Project BLUE BOOK / NARA

description: Circle-shaped object observed at Ramey AFB. Cross-referenced NUFORC ID 11902.
Circular object seen hovering silently over the water in line with the runway; shot upward and vanished.

U.S. Customs Inspector (age 74) and Pan Am employee (Santurce) observed object with fluttering descent; precise diameter described. Ramey AFB comms referenced. AF concluded “balloon possible” but testimony contradicted.
A farmer reported a dark, oval-shaped object descending and hovering over a sugarcane field. It emitted a high-pitched hum and bent the crop. Witnesses included family.
Two hikers saw a shiny disc following them for about a mile. The object made no noise and vanished behind a hill. Police confirmed the witnesses were visibly shaken.

A commercial airliner reported a “bright light” from an unknown object intermittently illuminating the cockpit as the craft passed close by Punta San Juan. The crew noted the object at close range before it departed.
Multiple drivers on the Mayagüez–Hormigueros road saw a pulsating light darting across the sky. Some described it as an orb. Vehicle radios experienced interference.

At 21:15 GMT, 40 officers and sailors from Command Task Group 45.2 observed a bluish-silver circular object over the sea. The object appeared larger than the moon, hovered for 10 seconds, then departed southwest. Total duration: 30 seconds. Logged in Project Blue Book files.
Radar tracked an object at Mach 10+ with abrupt stops.

Zulma Ramírez de Pérez family begins observing disc-shaped crafts (silvery metallic, translucid domes, colored lights, whooshing sound, figures visible inside domes) entering and exiting the waters of Laguna Cartagena. Family reports repeated sightings from this year forward. Earliest documented Laguna Cartagena USO entry on record.

Pan Am Flight 257 (Captain M. Van Winkle) en route from New York to San Juan encountered a fiery greenish-white round object. The pilot took violent evasive action as the UFO passed extremely close to the plane .

Pan American Airways pilot (Capt. Van Winkle, Flight 215-A) took violent evasive action as fiery round greenish-white object appeared on collision course at 3:33 AM. Several passengers thrown from seats. Object seen over wide area of eastern Caribbean.

location_name: Over Atlantic Ocean, en route San Juan, Puerto Rico (Pan Am Flight 257)
description: 4:30 AM. Captain Matthew Van Winkle, Pan Am Flight 257 from New York to San Juan, made violent evasive maneuver to avoid collision with a strange bolide heading toward airliner. Passengers and crew not wearing seatbelts hurled into bulkheads. Object described as 'shiny greenish core and outer ring that reflected the inner glow.' Pilot climbed 1,500 ft in seconds. Pilots from TransCaribbean Airways and other airlines 2-3 hundred miles away on same route saw the same object. TransCaribbean pilot John Walsh doubted it was a meteorite. Reported in San Juan's El Mundo newspaper. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Caribbean Crisis (2010).
AFWR radar tracked a high-speed UAP flying NW from Vieques toward Cabo Rojo. Object changed altitude rapidly and was estimated at Mach 3.
Object at 3,500 mph returned encrypted Mode 3 IFF—tracked near PR radar zone.
Marine training exercise halted after radar detected objects moving at 3,000+ mph.
Civilian witnesses reported a daylight unidentified aerial object over San Juan in the early 1960s.
A Venezuelan Super-Constellation at 10,000 ft encountered a glowing object that paralleled it for minutes before accelerating away.
Venezuelan Super-Constellation at 10,000 ft encountered a glowing object that paralleled aircraft for minutes before accelerating away. CIA document source: cia-rdp81r00560r000100010001-0.
Official Blue Book entry describing a UFO sighting near Ramey Air Force Base. Witness report describes a disc-like aerial object.
Official Project Blue Book entry describing UFO sighting near Ramey Air Force Base. Disc-like aerial object.
Project Blue Book report of aerial phenomenon over the capital city. Includes technical notations and witness drawings.

Multiple military and civilian sources observed a fast-moving intense light or missile-like object across Puerto Rico, and the Atlantic corridor. At 2335Z, aircraft over PR observed a light moving SSE. At 2350Z, Ramey AFB reported globular, flashing object. Submarine base in PR saw object with L - shaped lights and a halo-type cloud.
Civilian letter to DoD describing platinum star-shaped object with trailing red glow; rose into high altitude until lost from view. Blue Book Case File 8701081
Spinning disk of lights inside a “boiling cloud”; disk rose out and disappeared; coincided with power disruptions.
Oval luminous craft hovered over base perimeter, then accelerated west over the ocean.

During a US Navy antisubmarine exercise (commanded from carrier USS Wasp), a submarine’s sonar operator reported it breaking formation to pursue an unknown submerged object moving at over 150 knots. Multiple escort ships and patrol aircraft tracked this fast-moving underwater target for days (to depths ~27,000 ft)
Entry in Blue Book (Publication T1206). No incident number or narrative provided, but location explicitly listed as San Juan, Puerto Rico.

location_name: Waters off Puerto Rico (Springboard Exercises area)
description: During US Navy 'Springboard' exercises: one submarine broke away to pursue an unknown object moving at 150 knots (maximum submersible speed of the era was 45 knots). Fleet uncertain if it was a decoy or real anomaly. Object spent 4 days in the area, plunging to depths of 27,000 feet; 13 captains entered the incident in their ships' logs. USO communicated single-screw propulsion to COMLANT technicians. Object vanished after 96 hours. Source: Ivan Sanderson, Invisible Residents (1970); Inexplicata/Scott Corrales March 2012.

During a U.S. Navy anti-submarine warfare exercise off Puerto Rico, multiple ships and aircraft detected an underwater object moving far faster than any known sub—over 150 knots . Sonar contacts showed the unknown submersible maneuvering around the fleet and diving to an impossible depth of 27,000 feet . The task force tracked this super-fast “USO” for four days before the contact finally broke off .
FAA radar at Ponce tracked an intruding UAP over Navy-controlled airspace. The object refused IFF protocols and vanished suddenly.
NICAP 1964 UFO Evidence includes PR reports in comprehensive 575-case main chronology. Section XI PR sightings included among 46 states and DC in the 1964 compendium.

Quintín Ramírez (brother of Zulma Ramírez) encounters two tall blond beings (~6-7 ft) in tight silver suits emerging from Laguna Cartagena at Las Guanábanas dirt lane. Beings smiled and returned to lagoon when he asked them not to approach. Subsequent undisclosed additional encounters. Witness died before reporting publicly. Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP.
Bright metallic disc wobbling in night sky, then shot away at high speed.
USS Gyatt radar operators tracked a high-altitude, fast-moving object. No visual intercept. No precise behavior logged for this night, but this began a pattern of nightly radar detection.
Continued radar detection of the unknown aerial target by USS Gyatt. Object showed advanced maneuverability and erratic speeds.
Radar tracked an object traveling slowly, then increasing speed. Object passed over the USS Bainbridge at 180 knots. Intercept attempted, but object faded.

Lt. Cmdr. Kyle Woodbury (F-8, Roosevelt Roads) performed visual intercept of dark triangular craft. Object accelerated to over Mach 1, climbed beyond 50,000 ft. Navy radar confirmed maneuvers. Woodbury said F-8 was “hopelessly outclassed.”

USS Gyatt tracked an unidentified object on radar approaching from the northeast. An F‑8 Crusader was launched from Roosevelt Roads for interception. The object reportedly outpaced the fighter and ascended rapidly beyond its altitude ceiling. Radar screen photos were allegedly reviewed by the Foreign Technology Division (FTD).

U.S. Navy Atlantic Fleet Weapons Range tracked an unknown object on a NNE course (30°) from 19°07′N, 65°05′W to 19°52′N, 64°45′W. The object emitted encrypted IFF Mode 1 transponder signals. A DF‑8 fighter flying at Mach 0.99 (650 mph) at 45,000 ft attempted to intercept but was unable to follow as the object accelerated and ascended out of range.
Radar at Ramey AFB tracked two large disk-shaped UFOs. Navy A-4D jets were scrambled; the UFOs made impossible maneuvers before vanishing over the ocean.
Multiple disc-shaped craft in formation with rotating rim lights; sped off in unison.
Farmer claimed abduction by tall beings; burnt soil was documented.
Two Cessna pilots spotted an unidentified object while flying in formation north of Vega Baja.

Two USAF airmen (Sgts. Padilla and Henry) saw a large disc-shaped object hovering just above the ocean water off the NE corner of the base. The object had two distinct decks and bright lights; it moved side-to-side and then rose, before reversing course and disappearing. One airman photographed it. Air Force intelligence later judged the witnesses credible .
Francisco Soto Borges saw fleet of 7 disc-shaped craft in perfect formation flying soundlessly over Aguadilla coastal community.

Francisco Soto Borges observed fleet of seven disc-shaped craft in perfect formation flying soundlessly over the coastal community. He would experience a repeat event in May 1972. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, UFOs over Latin America and Spain.

During “Operation Racer Run,” the USS Waldron (DD-699) tracked two UAPs on radar with no IFF. Speed was ~400 mph, executing right-angle turns. Crew had visual contact at close range. One object submerged, then later emerged, joining the second before both departed upward at a 45° angle. Case reported to Washington.
Sgt. Carulli and 15 others spotted a UFO rising above the ocean, flashing bright strobe-like light at 02:30 AM.

7:30 PM. Uncle and nephew driving from Isabela to San Juan for New Year's gathering: car headlights illuminated a small entity with shining cat-like eyes standing next to a light-colored object on the roadside. As they slowed, the object rose from the ground. Both parties mutually startled. Generally acknowledged as the first UFO landing case in Puerto Rico. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Earth's Shadow Denizens (2012).

Two men saw a glowing object and one saw a short humanoid with glowing eyes. The object lit up blue-green, then red, and ascended. Report includes corroborated witness testimony and a signed military-style report.

Two Puerto Rico Air National Guard pilots chased a triangular UFO over San Juan. They “followed a ‘triangular shaped object, 50 feet in diameter’ for 20 minutes” before running low on fuel. The object was silent and large, witnesses reported.
USS Boxer radar crew tracked a craft accelerating to 4,200 mph and climbing to 90,000 ft. No sonic boom heard. Track lost after 2 minutes.

Pyramid-shaped object seen for several days over Puerto Rico, photographed, detected on radar, and observed from National Guard and other aircraft. Inquiry by Weather Bureau for Dr. James McDonald found no agency that had released such a balloon. Case referenced in Weinstein pilot-UFO catalogue. Source: UFO-Forschung/Weinstein catalogue analysis.
Fishermen reported a luminous object entering the water offshore near Aguada in the pre-1970 period.
Civilian witnesses reported an unidentified aerial object over the interior region during the mid-1970s; described as non-coastal.
A Caribair and Pan Am jet reported a UFO over Isla Verde. FAA later confirmed the event; a 1977 report acknowledged pilot sightings.
Fishermen reported luminous lights entering the sea offshore during the mid–late 1970s (Spanish-language reference).

Reynaldo Calzada, a U.S. Navy security supervisor for 35 years, reported witnessing a hovering disc-shaped craft with violet lights near an explosives storage area. Shortly after, a cigar-shaped object descended and absorbed the disc. Two more objects then emerged from the larger craft, circled around it, and disappeared. Jorge Martín believes this may have been part of a secret U.S. Navy program to monitor or initiate contact with non-human entities.
Observatory staff detected unusual microwave emissions during visual UAP sighting.
Group of nine campers saw 5–6 ft beings with long arms, large eyes, pointed ears. Entities glided silently between trees, leaving deep impressions on ground.
Structured craft seen from lighthouse and it was beaming light into the ocean.

~9:00 PM. Francisco Soto Borges on his porch observed a large light illuminate surroundings like helicopter searchlight. Circular craft partially concealed by cloud, only a few hundred feet up, with blue/red/golden lights streaming down to porch. A second vehicle appeared next to the first. Neighbors summoned to witness the display. Both craft disappeared without appearing to move from their positions. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, UFOs over Latin America and Spain.

Francisco Soto Borges (repeat witness) saw circular object partially concealed by cloud with blue/red/golden lights streaming down to his porch. Second vehicle appeared next to first. Both disappeared without moving from their positions. Multiple neighbors summoned to witness.

Francisco Soto Borges saw a large circular UFO partially hidden by a cloud. It emitted blue, red, and golden lights that bathed his porch in brilliance. A second vehicle appeared alongside it. Neighbors also witnessed the event. Borges believed the craft came from another dimension, as they vanished without moving.

During early morning guard duty, a very bright light appeared above the gatehouse, causing power and phone failure. The object vanished as quickly as it appeared, after which power and communication resumed. Reported internally. Logged in NUFORC (Oct 4, 2003) submission.
~20 workers (hospital & factory) and 4 police officers in Aguadilla reported multiple silent, disk-like craft overhead (each with a small cone on top and moving lights) .
Witnesses (including police) reported a spherical object with bright yellow lights floating over the mountains near Adjuntas for ~5 minutes.

location_name: Adjuntas-Dos Bocas road, near Utuado, Puerto Rico
description: ~9 p.m. Milton Kleber and nephew driving toward Dos Bocas lake saw oval "oyster-shaped" object flying parallel to their car at ~1000 ft elevation. Pale orange, matte, noiseless. When they stopped, object also halted and changed color from pale orange to bluish-green (mercury lamp color). Object responded to headlight flashing by pulsing its own light in slow-motion 4 times. Police in Utuado were simultaneously responding to multiple resident reports. Source: Sebastián Robiou, Manifiesto OVNI.

location_name: North of Adjuntas, road toward Dos Bocas (Utuado road), Puerto Rico
description: ~9:00 PM. Milton Kleber and nephew driving toward Dos Bocas hydroelectric lake observed rounded flattened object ('like an oyster') flying parallel to vehicle at ~1000 ft elevation, pale orange matte color. Object matched vehicle's speed. When Kleber pulled over, object halted and changed to bluish-green (mercury lamppost color) then shifted to 11-o'clock position before turning orange again. Kleber flashed headlights; object responded by disappearing and reappearing 4 times in succession, slowly absorbing and re-emitting its own light 'like pulses in slow motion.' Drove to Utuado where police were already responding to calls from residents about objects in the sky. Source: Sebastián Robiou Lamarche, Manifiesto OVNI; Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2015.

Milton Kleber and his nephew witnessed a matte, pale-orange oyster-shaped object moving parallel to their vehicle. The UFO changed color to bluish-green and seemed to react to their car headlights, dimming and then pulsing light in response.

Newspaper El Mundo published photos allegedly taken in Adjuntas linked to teenage contactee in Ponce who claimed telepathic communication. Arecibo Atmospheric Observatory issued dismissal attributing sightings to atmospheric gas releases.
Adjuntas Mayor Rigoberto Ramos and others saw three disc-shaped lights in the sky; the lights changed intensity and color as the objects moved rapidly overhead .

A factory worker reported seeing oval, orange–silver lights, noiseless, crossing the mountains. An agricultural worker described objects like the sun moving at average speed before vanishing. A college student described a high-altitude “coffin-shaped” object seen from his grandmother’s home.
Nino González experienced a CE-2: his television and household appliances went dark as a “flying saucer” passed overhead.

Series of UFO sightings causing widespread public commotion. Newspaper El Nuevo Día reported objects described as orange-yellow, appearing and vanishing on regular schedule 7-10 p.m. Police commander Luis Maldonado Trinidad forwarded report to superiors: balloon-shaped yellow lights at considerable speed, noiseless, luminosity 50-60 seconds. Mayor Rigoberto Ramos witnessed three discs resembling full moon. Electric appliances failed during close approach. Multiple witnesses including priest, school principal, merchants, farmers. Gordon Creighton (FSR) noted proximity to high-radioactivity soil zones and proximity to reservoirs, dams, electric plants, high-voltage power lines.
William Serrano reported seeing a light approach as he walked home. Lights in his house browned-out as the object neared. When it headed directly toward him, he dove into brushwood to avoid it.

Three-week UFO flap over Adjuntas and surrounding area, Oct 1972. Objects described as orange-yellow, noiseless, appearing nightly between 7-10 PM on regular schedule, varying in intensity, vanishing into mountains. Mayor Rigoberto Ramos personally witnessed three discs resembling the full moon over the mountains. Police Commander Luis Maldonado Trinidad filed report to superiors: balloon-shaped yellow lights, considerable speed, 50-60 second luminosity bursts, moving side to side. Factory worker saw oval/orange/silver objects; agricultural worker described sun-like objects; college student saw coffin-shaped object. Electric appliances in homes browned-out when objects flew overhead (Nino González CE-2). William Serrano jumped into brushwood when object headed straight at him. Researchers Noel Rigau and Sebastián Robiou interviewed dozens of witnesses including Mayor Ramos, town priest, school principal. Stanton Friedman visited without obtaining satisfactory evidence. Gordon Creighton (FSR) noted correlation with USGS gamma aeroradioactivity zones and proximity to reservoirs, dams, electric plants, power lines, and radio installations. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2015; Robiou Lamarche Manifiesto OVNI; Gordon Creighton FSR.
Police and civilians reported balloon-shaped yellow lights appearing nightly over the mountains. These sightings formed part of a broader Adjuntas/Utuado UFO wave.

location_name: Arecibo Shopping Center warehouse, Arecibo, Puerto Rico
description: 4:00 AM. Anonymous truck driver and assistant making a delivery, resting in truck. Driver feigned sleep and observed three whitish beings ~normal height with close-fitting helmets with a crest front-to-back, parrot-beak hooked noses, scaled outfits, peering into truck window. Beings communicated in bat-squeaking sounds. Driver developed high fever and headaches for two days after encounter, reported sensation of cranial pressure while observing beings. Investigated by Sebastián Robiou (interviewed witness January 1974). Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2012, Robiou Lamarche.

During the 1973 U.S. mainland UFO flap, students and faculty at UPR Mayagüez observed a large disk-shaped craft with brilliant lights hovering over campus. Witnesses described it as “scanning” the area. Reported in local media at the time.

location_name: Flamingo Terrace development, Bayamón, Puerto Rico
description: Several weeks before the January 3, 1974 Flamingo Terrace UFO sighting, local children reported encountering a bizarre monkey-like being in the woods surrounding the development. Whether this creature was linked to the subsequent UFO sighting was never determined. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2023.

While driving a 1963 Impala on the Sabana Grande–Maricao road, witnesses encountered a brilliant aerial light that caused a complete vehicle shutdown. Headlights dimmed, the engine stalled, and after a blinding flash directly overhead, the car’s battery and wiring were found melted and destroyed.

Amid a regional UFO flap (with cattle mutilations and strange lights reported), a group of 9 campers on an overnight UFO vigil in El Yunque National Forest experienced a terrifying encounter with multiple humanoid creatures. The intruders, described as 5–6 vaguely humanoid figures with clawed hands and large ears, surrounded the campers for hours during the night . One camper got within 10 ft of a creature and even touched it. At dawn the beings vanished; the campers found oversized footprints and made plaster casts as evidence (though these were later stolen) . A Puerto Rican TV documentary later featured their case.

Housewife hanging laundry observed silver hoop object tilting in sky. Intense buzzing sound inside home rose to explosive head pressure. Compelled to window, confronted by tall humanoid with tight silver outfit, red uncovered face, no nose or mouth, large hollow dark eye sockets (eyeless). Entity demanded she give him her stone mashing implement; when she did, entity recoiled and ordered 'not to look at him.' Entity telepathically communicated words 'cerakia ovint.' Neighbors heard buzzing at same time but saw nothing. Hypnotic regression conducted 1975 by Dr. Manuel Méndez del Toro. First hypnotic regression used on UFO percipient in Puerto Rico. Investigated by CEOVNI (William Santana). First famous PR CE-3 humanoid case. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2012, Robiou Lamarche, Contactos Extraterrestres 1981.

location_name: Barrio Obrero, San Juan, Puerto Rico
description: Corrections officer I. Simonetti saw brass-colored object in pendular motion before taking off north. Neighbor on rooftop photographed it. Barrio Obrero photographs show turreted object similar to McMinnville, Oregon photo. Forwarded to CEOVNI.

Farmers Luis Torres, his son José Daniel, and neighbors saw a huge silver craft, house-sized, flying at treetop level only ~100 yards away. Lights resembled those of a police car. Linked to the onset of the Moca Vampire animal deaths wave. CEOVNI investigated.
During a rum commercial shoot, film captured a bright UFO appearing after an airliner passed. Object moved off toward El Yunque. Featured in 1975 doc Platillos Voladores Sobre Puerto Rico.

Puerto Rico's southwestern corner was scene of UFO and religious phenomena. Devotees of Our Lady of Sábana Grande reported miracles. CEOVNI subsequently investigated animal deaths on western side of island linked to UFOs.

8:10 p.m. Ten families (12+ individuals) saw slightly flattened circular object flying low over development. CEOVNI investigator William Santana conducted thorough interviews. All witnesses agreed on description. Children reported monkey-like being in nearby woods weeks before.

Around 8:00 PM, numerous residents (adults, children, elderly) saw a domed UFO move east to west at low altitude. It hovered near a forested plateau. Later, children witnessed it emit a bright beam, releasing five glowing green spheres. A blue light shone through a square opening on its side. The object released a white smoke-like substance and hovered over a nearby structure before departing. Witnesses described a low humming sound and said the object behaved as if it were “observing.”

Shortly before 8 PM. Lizzy Rodríguez de Maisonet, her husband (former commercial airline pilot), neighbor Iris Esther Rivera, and children (Richard, Raymond, Ronnie - ages 8-10): saw flat-bottomed round object with upper protuberance (inverted soup dish shape) moving E to W slower than normal aircraft, until it disappeared behind tree-covered hill. After adults went inside, the three Rivera children and others observed the object return at ground level near the hill/ravine behind Guillermina Street. Craft hovered over Miguel Meléndez School plateau. Investigation by CEOVNI researcher Noel E. Rigau (1976), published in Inexplicata IHU 50-year retrospective.

location_name: Flamingo Terrace development, Bayamón, Puerto Rico
description: 8:10 PM. Ten families, over a dozen individuals of various ages, reported seeing a slightly flattened circular object flying low over Flamingo Terrace. CEOVNI investigator William Santana conducted thorough interviews with witnesses who agreed in description. Note: Only a few weeks prior, local children claimed to have encountered a bizarre monkey-like being in surrounding woods. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2023. (Note: detailed version from Nexusnewsfeed already in registry as Jan 3, 1974 Flamingo Terrace.)

location_name: Barrio Obrero, San Juan, Puerto Rico
description: Corrections officer I. Simonetti walking home observed a brass-colored object swinging in pendular motion in the sky, initially thought to be a kite, before it took off northward. People on the street shouting and pointing at sky. A neighbor on a rooftop photographed the object. Simonetti obtained copies and forwarded them to CEOVNI: photographs depict a black, turreted object. Rooftops, palm trees, and streetlights visible in photo allow altitude determination. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2023.

Corrections officer I. Simonetti observed a brass-colored object swinging in pendulum motion before taking off north. Crowds in the street pointed at it; a neighbor photographed it. Photos showed an object similar to the McMinnville UFO, with rooftops, palm trees, and streetlights giving altitude reference.

During a 24-hour time-lapse commercial shoot from the rooftop of the Sheraton Hotel in San Juan, a luminous object was captured accelerating eastward toward El Yunque. The phenomenon was only noticed during playback. The footage—long presumed lost or inaccessible—has resurfaced in part on YouTube.

CEOVNI investigation (William Santana, architect) conducted in 1975: confirmed neighbors heard the intense buzzing sound during the December 23, 1973 Barrio Limón CE-3 encounter but saw nothing unusual. Research confirmed this was the first use of hypnotic regression on a UFO percipient in Puerto Rico. Also confirmed: investigator Carlos Busquets catalogued parapsychological development in witness after encounter. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2012.

CEOVNI researchers (investigating UFO-animal mutilation connection) interviewed Luis Torres, his son José Daniel, and neighbors who witnessed a UFO near Moca during 1975 animal mutilation wave. Object described as silver-colored, size of a house, with lights resembling a police squad car, flying at treetop level and as close as 100 yards from witnesses. Preceded the 'Moca Vampire' cases. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2023.

Farmer Luis Torres, son José Daniel, and neighbors saw silver-colored craft as large as a house crossing sky at treetop level ~100 yards away. Lights resembled police squad car lights. CEOVNI investigation linked to Moca Vampire animal mutilation wave.
Navy divers encountered a USO that disabled their equipment.
Farmer Félix Badillo found piglet dead with a precise surgical incision on its ear; vet Ángel de la Sierra said wound looked “experimental.”
Juan Muñiz confronted a feathered creature near his home; threw rocks, then armed with machete. Entity fled into darkness.
Eight goats and twelve rabbits mutilated. Watchmen heard a deafening noise, then saw a shadowy figure running through trees.

DJ Willy Lopez felt knocks on the studio door and saw a luminous figure on the terrace. He then observed a yellow‑white saucer ≈40 ft in diameter hovering above the cooling tower; the terrace grew unnaturally hot and civil‑defense personnel later checked radiation levels .

Orlando Franceschi, ambulance driver, confronted a 5-ft tall humanoid with long ears, long nose, lipless mouth, grey skin, “black spot” eyes, ape-like jawline. Attacked it with a shovel twice with no effect. On third swing, he became numb and paralyzed. Entity then faded into thin air.

location_name: Fort San Cristóbal, Old San Juan / Condado, San Juan / Cupey
description: A cigar-shaped craft hovered 1,500 ft above Cupey for 45 min; other luminous objects seen over Old San Juan and Condado. Silent, widely observed.

Captain, OOD, CICWO, and bridge crew aboard the USS Steinaker (DD-863) observed a large aerial object (~300 ft wide), glowing with what resembled roiling lightning. The ship passed beneath it at ~100 ft below cloud level. Not detected on radar. Described as brighter than daylight. Initially suspected to be “ball lightning,” but no atmospheric or electrical explanation fit. Reported to MUFON.
Funeral photo revealed a dark oval UFO above mourners, unseen at time of exposure. Image published in La Semana newspaper.

9:30 AM. Witness alone in kitchen: tall blond man in light tight-fitting outfit with red light at waist appeared, communicated telepathically 'not to be afraid.' Witness blacked out, awakened in large silvery oval room with similar beings and computer terminals. Examined by blond beings and shorter large-headed grey being using brilliant blue light. Source: Robiou Lamarche, Manifiesto OVNI en Puerto Rico Santo Domingo Y Cuba.

Afternoon. Man walking on wooded hillside saw large silvery cylinder/'elevator' emerge from ground. Four tall blond men in tight blue uniforms emerged, followed by a taller hulking dark-haired figure. Blond men searched area while hairy being followed obediently. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.
Multiple witnesses reported a green, cigar-shaped luminous object floating silently over the rainforest canopy, then vanishing.

A man observed a 3.5 ft humanoid in a green inflated suit with a helmet and lights. The being flew away using a jetpack-like device after being startled. At least 10 additional witnesses saw the lights in nearby trees. Large tracks were found and photographed.

8:30 PM. Adrian De Olmos Ordones (42) observed 3.5 ft humanoid in green inflated garment with metallic glass-front helmet, four-fingered hands, duck-like feet, tail, and backpack. Being retreated when outside light turned on, pressed belt control activating backpack with red/blue lights and electric drill sound, rose 10 ft and flew horizontally to grove 150 yards away. Family and neighbors observed lights moving in trees for 10 mins, joined by second light. Cattle on adjacent farm went wild. Tracks found and photographed next day. Investigated by Sebastián Robiou Lamarche; reported in Flying Saucer Review. URECAT-000217. Sources: Robiou Lamarche, Berlitz, Jorge Martín, Richard Hall.

9:15 AM. Young secretary heard strange sound and found two small men (~3 ft tall) studying a map of Puerto Rico hanging on wall. Beings had long arms, short legs, large flat-backed heads, large ears, two small antennae, wore shiny transparent green outfits. Second witness saw beings exit through window. Source: Robiou Lamarche, Manifiesto OVNI en Puerto Rico Santo Domingo Y Cuba.
~2000 witnesses saw two large glowing yellow-orange orbs drift over the city, settle ~1 mile offshore in the Atlantic, repeatedly enter/exist the water (USO/UFO).

Afternoon. Luis Sandoval resting on a hill overlooking north coast: heard loud report and saw a huge ball of light hurtle toward him. Ball faded to reveal a small creature (~child-sized) with yellowish skin, large black eyes, unpleasant features, dressed in a grey tuxedo, yellow shirt, white collar, and green cravat, wearing a helmet. Being announced it was an 'ultraterrestrial being,' then examined Sandoval with a stethoscope-like device (feet, knees, chest, back, temples, head). When exam finished, being turned and was re-enveloped in the light sphere which departed. Sandoval's dog howled throughout and was found dead days later. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Earth's Shadow Denizens (2012).

Traveling salesman Edwin Almenas encountered a pillar of flame/fireball on the roadside that killed his car’s electrical systems. The object emitted golden-orange beams. Terrified, he watched it rise and vanish; afterward the car restarted. The next evening, on his return to San Juan, he again experienced voices in his head, another car shutdown, and saw a fireball. A 3-ft-tall humanoid figure enveloped in light appeared, holding “two rings” and a flashlight-like device. It pointed toward El Torito mountain, merged back into the fireball, and departed. Almenas later reported neck and head pain.

Adrián Olmos (same witness as July 12 Quebradillas case) was making deliveries when he heard squealing/shrieking sounds inside his head (like monkey shrieks, later like record played at extreme speed). Severe throbbing headache and heat sensation forced him to stop. He observed a 13-ft diameter ball of light blocking road composed of many smaller lights. From the ball emerged two small lizard-like beings with apple-colored faces, platinum-white outfits, diamond-like structures on foreheads with black headbands, four-fingered hands, small boots, and one carrying a monitoring box. Beings shrieked at each other; when Olmos challenged them they turned toward him, shrieked 'as if wanting to explain something,' then were enveloped in cloudiness from the lights and vanished. Ball rose into sky. Physical aftermath: dazed, whole body ached. Olmos subsequently sold his home in Quebradillas, developed panic attacks. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2012, Robiou Lamarche.
Object descended at 14,000 mph then stopped

Two UFOs seen by multiple witnesses emerging from and re-entering the sea. One object hovered silently before slowly descending into the water. Shortly after, a second object followed the same path. Local authorities and residents observed.

2:15 AM. Heart patient on terrace saw three approaching flickering multicolored lights descend, then transform into three small 3-ft figures in blue tight diving suits. Beings spoke in rapid high-pitched tone, one placed hand on witness's chest with flickering lights. Source: Jorge Martín/Robiou Lamarche/Enigma.

Night. Man noticed two tall light-skinned men with long blond-silver hair standing next to trees, wearing long black tunics with large multicolored medallions. Beings stared briefly then walked away and slowly disappeared from view. Source: Jorge Martín, Enigma #43.

Minister Jose Manuel Rodriguez reported repeated encounters with humanoid beings from the planet Koshnak, near the Orion constellation. He described them as having melon-shaped faces, green eyes, and telepathic communication abilities. Rodriguez claimed the beings provided spiritual messages and warnings. His multiple encounters were documented in detail, and his testimony was consistent over time.

2 witnesses (mother & daughter) experienced a grisly abduction/NHI event: driving on Hwy 2, the car lost power and everything went dark; they saw a bluish-white beam and strange humanoids (6 ft, pale-grey “greys”) in the car, then regained consciousness 2½ hours later near Arecibo.
Fisherman José “Chino” Zaya and a friend encountered several small, gray beings in a cave. One was killed and preserved; body later shown to UPR personnel.

location_name: Cerro Las Tetas (Tetas de Cayey), Salinas, Puerto Rico
description: Night fishing. José 'Chino' Zaya and friend discovered series of caves near Cerro Las Tetas. Small beings (~several) were occupying one of the caves. One creature grabbed Zaya by the leg; he killed it with a stick and preserved the body in formaldehyde. Police officer Osvaldo Santiago examined and photographed the body. Prof. Calixto Peres (UPR chemistry) examined it and stated: 'In my opinion it is something extraterrestrial.' Body later examined by University of Puerto Rico. Case investigated for 2 years by Jorge Martín.

A U.S. Navy lookout saw an underwater unidentified structure: a round assembly of red, green and white lights (~6 ft across) maneuvering near the ship’s sonar tow-cable. It hovered for ~20 minutes then disappeared

While jogging at around 9:00 AM near the mangroves and salt flats of Tallaboa (between Ponce and Peñuelas), Héctor Maldonado witnessed 5–6 greyish-blue humanoid beings (5–6 ft tall, bald, glowing almond-shaped eyes, long arms) abducting a human man in a checkered cream suit. The man appeared unconscious and was lifted by his armpits. A large, silent, metallic oval craft with a cupola and Christmas-like lights hovered above the sea nearby. One alien mocked Maldonado telepathically, displayed unusual teeth, ran at superhuman speed, and attempted to subdue him with a cold liquid projected at his chest, causing temporary paralysis. He escaped by continuing to run. Years later, Maldonado found a 100-ft-wide circular ground depression at the same spot and witnessed Civil Defence personnel investigating it. Multiple other UFO sightings were later confirmed in the same area by others.
source_citation: Flying Saucer Review V42N4 (1997); Originally from Evidencia OVNI No. 1 via Jorge Martin https://avalonlibrary.net/ebooks/Timothy%20Good%20-%20Unearthly%20Disclosure.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com

description: Night. 18-year-old alone at home: two tall thin beings with large heads and eyes appeared, paralyzed him, and carried him aboard large oval craft with flange. Taken to undersea city/installation, medically examined and cured of ulcers and hemorrhoids, then transported back home. Animals subsequently react violently in his presence. Source: Jorge Martín, Enigma #1.

location_name: Tallaboa (Ponce Salt Flats area), Puerto Rico
description: 9:00 AM. Man jogging near salt flats saw several beings (~5-6, 6 ft tall, very thin, large bald heads, large slanted luminous eyes, gray-blue skin, long thin arms, sharp teeth) apparently struggling with a dark-haired man in stupor, attempting to carry him to shoreline where metallic domed oval craft hovered silently. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.

location_name: El Combate, Cabo Rojo, Puerto Rico
description: Night. Couple in beachfront cabin saw bright multicolored oval object hovering above water ½ mile offshore, emitting some type of field. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.

A large object with multiple lights was reportedly seen by hundreds of witnesses across Puerto Rico. A police officer managed to photograph the object. The object was described as massive and clearly visible in the night sky. Reports describe the lights as bright and fixed on the underside of the craft. Eyewitnesses included residents and police personnel, and the incident generated wide discussion. No follow-up official investigation is cited, but the photograph and testimonies were circulated in local media.

While deployed, the USS W. S. Sims (DE-1059) crew was instructed to monitor an underwater contact of unknown origin. Divers reported it was “another Shag Harbour,” a term the sailor didn’t understand at the time. The official story changed repeatedly—from an unknown craft to a Soviet sub to a “natural phenomenon.” Details remain ambiguous.
Hundreds of witnesses observed a disc with bright lights flying very low over Levittown. Police Sgt. Cordero photographed it on Polaroid as it passed directly overhead.
Navy MH-53E pilot observed a dark mass under water and later saw a torpedo pulled beneath the surface near the same location. Potential high-speed submerged anomaly.
Civilian witnesses reported an unidentified aerial object over the south-central interior near the Toa Vaca area during the 1980s.

Nancy Alvarado reported that while waiting in her car she heard a voice in her head saying “don’t be afraid.” She then saw two human-like figures—one in white, one in dark green—gliding just above the ground. Both had black hair, small shiny eyes, and tight one-piece suits. After gazing at her, they crossed the street toward a wooded field and vanished.

Two Puerto Rican men, Jose Maldonado Torres and Jose Pagan Santos, disappeared while flying Ercoupe N3808H from Santo Domingo to San Juan. At ~8:03 p.m., they issued a Mayday reporting a “weird object” altering their course multiple times and causing navigational failure. An Iberia flight relayed their transmissions. Radar contact was lost ~35 miles west of Puerto Rico. No wreckage was ever found.
Civilian witnesses reported an unidentified aerial object over the interior mountainous region during the late 1980s; no coastal association mentioned.

Ranch owner and two friends found cattle repeatedly mutilated under mysterious circumstances. While guarding the ranch at night, they saw four short gray large-headed creatures emerge from a nearby ravine and approach the cattle. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.
Residents reported intense subterranean buzzing and drilling noises over several nights. Public protests occurred before all coverage was abruptly dropped.

Three hunters camping in isolated El Yunque mountain area heard noises, then saw four dark figures with bright red eyes emerge from woods. The creatures walked toward witnesses, who fired rifles without apparent effect. Beings grabbed one witness, tore his shirt, punched him twice, then vanished. Source: Jorge Martín, Enigma #34.

Witnesses saw a being that ran at incredible speed among brush and trees, breaking tree branches in half as it ran. The being was observed entering the nearby ocean and disappearing. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs/Robiou Lamarche.

At ~23:40, the USS John King (DDG-3) bridge crew, including the QMOW, observed six uniform, glowing green circular formations beneath the water. The ship passed over three of them, illuminating the ocean bottom (~20 ft draft). Sea state was calm, moonless, and clear. OOD logged it as “glowing algae,” but no marine bioluminescence matched the event.

Two local girls (Migdalia Cintrón and Aida Rivera), involved in previous encounters, again encountered a beautiful female figure that held on firmly to them and told them she needed them to come with her. Possible religious/contact phenomenon. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.
Anomalous radio signal coincided with UAP sighting

location_name: Paso Palma, Barrio Utuado, Puerto Rico
description: UFO/entity encounter. Exact details truncated in search snippet. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs 1983 June sightings page.

A police officer saw a glowing light descending into the El Yunque rainforest; immediately his patrol car’s engine and lights went out. Authorities found no debris. Later the Forest Service suggested it may have been a meteor .

location_name: El Yunque (La Coca Waterfall / El Toro Mountain), Puerto Rico
description: US Army special forces unit on classified mission in El Yunque encounters unknown entity near La Coca waterfall. Jeep and all electronic equipment fail (EM effect). Unit fires on approaching entity after warnings ignored; 3 rounds apparently hit target. Large green luminescent fluid trail found by personnel in anti-contamination suits from Roosevelt Roads Naval Station. Soldiers debriefed at Roosevelt Roads, records classified/altered. Feb 19: alleged UFO crash in El Yunque slope, military secures entire area. Source: high-ranking US military officer confidential statement to Jorge Martín.

location_name: La Coca Waterfall, Sierra de Luquillo
description: After a UFO allegedly crashed into a slope of El Yunque, witnesses reported U.S. military retrieval of the craft and bodies. A High-ranking U.S. officer: 15 soldiers & 3 officers encountered unknown presence; equipment failure; footsteps and firing; trail of green luminescent “blood” after entity shot. Specialized units from Roosevelt Roads arrived. Records altered.
Multiple witnesses reported a “cigar-shaped mothership” releasing smaller orbs.
Angel Bernard and son disappeared for 4 days; claimed encounter with a red-eyed humanoid surrounded by children. Reported time distortion and sudden onset of nightfall.

location_name: Maguayo community, near Laguna Cartagena, Lajas, Puerto Rico
description: Carlitos Muñoz family: large platform/carrier object hovered over Laguna Cartagena with yellow and green lights. Multiple smaller luminous objects emerged from underside, flew in different directions, re-entered the large object which departed upward. Observed regularly. Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP.

Marines at Roosevelt Roads observed one or more disc-shaped craft overhead. A-7 fighter jets were scrambled to intercept and reportedly photographed one “disc.” The military gave no official explanation .
Radio telescope detected prime‑number sequence
U.S. Marine personnel reported luminous objects entering/exiting the sea. Navy submarines and helicopters deployed. Considered a strong USO case.

Residents witnessed red ball coming out of sky and breaking the waters of Laguna Cartagena. Event described as "controlled." Hours later at 2 a.m. May 31, a large cylinder-shaped object seen. Large fissures appeared in ground around Lajas and Cabo Rojo emitting cobalt-colored smoke. Military personnel in anti-contamination suits collected samples. Laguna Cartagena cordoned off. Investigated by Jorge Martin beginning summer 1987. Source: UFO Insight/Jorge Martin.
Explosion and earthquake reported near Laguna Cartagena, military presence, UFO sightings over lagoon on June 3–5.

location_name: Maguayo community, near Laguna Cartagena, Lajas, Puerto Rico
description: Large red ball of fire flew and made turns over lagoon, descended slowly and controlled into the lagoon waters with buzzing sound. At 2:00 AM: huge flying saucer hovering low over lagoon covered with bright white light, circling slowly ~2 min before departing at high speed. Next day at 1:55 PM: the Laguna Cartagena tremor/explosion (originally reported epicenter 81,000 ft deep under lagoon). Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP.

Large cylinder-shaped object with spheres at each end observed by many residents in Betances community. Craft hovered silently for ~15 minutes before speeding away to the south. Came within 24 hours of Laguna Cartagena event. Source: UFO Insight.

location_name: Laguna Cartagena / Betances / Maguayo, Lajas, Puerto Rico
description: 10:00 PM June 1-3 (three consecutive nights): Huge cylindrical object with greenish-white ball of light at each end + revolving reddish underside light hovered over lagoon ~500 ft. Witnesses: Carlos Mercado, wife Haydée Álvarez, Rosa Acosta. Object motionless 15 min then departed south. 38 circular ground marks (35-40 ft dia) found in Olivares sector afterwards, with pH changes in soil rims. Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP.

location_name: Olivares sector, behind Sierra Bermeja, Lajas, Puerto Rico
description: Dolán Acosta on balcony engulfed by bright beam from above: could see own skeleton through body like an X-ray. Sister Eunice witnessed same. Both could see each other's bones and empty eye sockets. Lasted ~5 min. No after-effects. Small glowing beings occasionally seen in area disappearing when approached. Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP.

location_name: Las Tres T area, El Yunque, Puerto Rico
description: Four Puerto Rico National Guard members (off-duty) and three children encountered small Grey alien (~3-4 ft) while shrimp fishing in El Yunque at ~9 p.m. Being made wavy-gait walk, had egg-like head, dark eyes, skinny body, membrane connecting torso to upper arms. Flying saucer descended from El Yunque mountain, landed; 2 more Greys emerged and flanked the first. All witnesses paralyzed. Beings spoke in Spanish with strange accent before re-entering craft, which departed toward antenna sector at El Yunque summit. Source: senior PRNG officer confidential statement to Jorge Martín.

Multiple witnesses reported a large object descending from the sky followed by a bright explosion and cobalt-blue smoke. Days later, witnesses saw federal agents, trucks, and unmarked vehicles in the area. Burned vegetation and signs of disturbance were photographed. Researcher Jorge Martín investigated and documented local testimony, claiming the area may be connected to deep underground structures.

Multiple USO sightings in Laguna Cartagena area documented by researcher Jorge Martin. Objects observed entering and exiting lagoon. Area cordoned off by US military. Regarded as one of Caribbean's primary USO hotspots. Investigated by Jorge Martin with eyewitness interviews 1987-1995.
Jorge R. Rodríguez Vázquez reported a house-sized glowing object in the Salinas area during the 1988–1991 window. Exact date unresolved.
Coast Guard intercepted a distress call about a “black triangle.”

Strong underground explosion felt and heard in Lajas-Cabo Rojo area. Described by radio broadcaster Raymond Stewart (Super B Radio, Lajas) as 'Incredible.' Part of series of explosion events tied to Laguna Cartagena UFO activity beginning May 31, 1987.
Amaury Rivera reported abduction and photographed disc-shaped craft with military jets. Photos circulated widely; case discussed in conferences.
Residents & fishermen: huge luminous craft emerged from sea near the PR Trench, hovered, then ascended rapidly. Multiple corroborating witnesses.

location_name: Cabo Rojo / Sierra Bermeja, Puerto Rico
description: Multiple witnesses reported US military jets apparently vanishing into huge triangular objects on two separate occasions. Increased activity in Salinas where US military installation is located. Source: UFO Insight/Jorge Martin.

A witness on a survivalist camping trip saw multicolored lights 60 ft away. A flash followed, revealing a tall man (~6’2”) with long blond-white hair in a white outfit. The witness was paralyzed as the being told him to stop crying, claimed to be on a peace mission, and drew a circle-triangle-human symbol on the ground before disappearing behind a rock.
Giant UFO observed descending into swamp; ‘elevator’ with humanoids and robotic creature seen by Melvin Rosado.

location_name: Between San Germán and Sabana Grande mountains
description: Two U.S. Navy jet fighters reportedly vanished over mountainous terrain. No official explanation or wreckage confirmed. Eyewitnesses reported aerial activity.
Hundreds saw massive triangular craft. Two Navy F-14s approached and were allegedly absorbed by the object. Jets later reported lost at sea.

Over 100 witnesses reported massive silent triangular craft hovering above Sierra Bermeja mountains. Three US Navy F-14 jets scrambled. As jets approached, craft split into two separate lights disappearing in opposite directions. One jet allegedly disappeared (unconfirmed). Widely cited as most credible Puerto Rico sighting of the decade. Source: Going Coastal research.
Fisherman‑diver reports encountering a glowing, saucer‑shaped craft underwater. Allegedly saw it move, glow, then disappear with no plausible explanation offered.

Circular holes (~30 inches) were found in vegetation along roadsides in Juana Díaz and Guánica. Investigators (agrónomo, físico, and A. Álvarez) reported unusually high radiation and magnetism at the sites .

Maria and Jerry (surnames withheld) were in El Yunque when a pale blond man, ~6 ft tall, appeared beside their car. He spoke in an American-accented monotone, wore a colorful Hawaiian shirt, walked stiffly, and later requested a ride to Fajardo. He hardly moved or spoke; after being dropped near abandoned houses, he vanished down an empty street. Witnesses realized later that communication had been telepathic.

A young man identified as “Raul” and two friends were camping in El Yunque when they heard a strange humming sound and saw a metallic object hovering above the trees. It emitted a bright beam of light that caused panic. According to the witnesses, two humanoid beings with glowing eyes emerged near their campsite. The campers fled in fear. Raul later claimed to have experienced sleep disturbances, vivid dreams, and what he believed were missing time episodes.
23 witnesses saw a huge silent boomerang-shaped craft (~300 ft × 50 ft, dark silvery-gray) hovering in the rainforest with pulsating yellow-orange lights . 23

Fisherman Orlando Cataquet saw bright white lights near the shore, chased them, and encountered two identical tall men with light skin, blue eyes, and light-brown shoulder-length hair wearing white tunics. They warned him of a coming global freeze and ordered him to turn around; a flash of light followed, and they vanished. He later recalled three hours of missing time and glimpsed a long, submarine-like craft drawing power from the sea.

location_name: Northwestern Puerto Rico coastal waters (near Aguadilla)
description: Afternoon. Inocencio Cataquet, expert skin-diver from northwestern Puerto Rico, fishing when he observed a huge object moving underwater, estimated ~200 ft in diameter. He plunged in and touched it — not metallic, but porous and filled with small holes. Water around the object began heating up; object then moved away at high speed underwater and broke the surface, ascending skyward as 'a tiny little point of light.' Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Ships and Saucers (2012).

location_name: Adjuntas, Puerto Rico (copper mine test pits)
description: Connection noted between UFO activity and copper mine test pits near Adjuntas (Kennecott Copper operations). UFO sightings drawn to mining operations. Specifically mentioned in context of PR/Adjuntas skywatch recordings 1990-91. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Neglected Lights: Mexico's 1970s Saucer Wave (Jan 2012).

Skywatch recordings by Willie Durand Urbina/Puerto Rican Research Group. Audio captures live witnesses describing triangular object from Utuado area stopping over Pellejas, heading to Juan González then Jayuya. Object waited for commercial airliners to pass before emerging. Aircraft (non-commercial) reconnoitered the unknown object. Object ascended toward Cerro Maravilla.

A U.S. Navy CH-53 Sea Stallion crew on a mission to recover a BQM drone witnessed a large, dark mass underwater that pulled the drone down. The diver was lifted out safely, and the object descended back into the depths. Report relayed by Commander David Fravor (2019 interview).

Witnesses reported triangular objects flashing lights and flying near airplanes. An audio recording described: “A triangular object is above [an airplane], flashing its lights. The plane is trying to find it.”
Visitors reported a Bigfoot-like hairy being wearing a “belt of light.” Witnesses described it as aggressive and violent compared to earlier “manimal” reports.
Large triangular craft, silent, flying low over runway. Military police restricted access. Witnesses described it as “blacker than night sky.”

location_name: South of Navy firing range, Vieques, Puerto Rico
description: Fisherman Aníbal Corcino, his father, and Carlos Zenón (later imprisoned for trespassing firing range) observed enormous disc-shaped saucer with colored lights hovering ~100 ft over sea, drawing seawater up through underside green light in large column without sound. Object departed at high speed toward El Yunque. Second event: huge green light rose from seafloor to surface, object emerged silently into sky with drizzle of water. Corroborated by many other Vieques fishermen. Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP Vieques book.
Veteran fisherman Alicio Ayala observed a large, glowing object pacing his boat before descending silently into the sea near a deep trench.

Watchman Heriberto Acosta saw a huge whitish object shaped like a giant turtle shell, illuminated from below. Hovered silently ~60 ft above him. He was terrified but observed until it suddenly ascended and vanished.
[Other] Laguna Carta

UFO activity returned to central mountains. Large spherical object disgorged smaller ones vanishing westward. Watchman Heriberto Acosta (Lajas municipal dump) observed turtle-shell shaped craft at 60 ft altitude — white underneath, cast light like daytime, no visible lights, sudden ascent. Police received multiple calls. Source: Inexplicata/IHU, El Vocero.

location_name: Adjuntas and Utuado, Puerto Rico (Barrio Pellejas, Barrio Guilarte)
description: UFO flap returns to central mountains, following renewed Cabo Rojo activity in late 1980s. Residents of Adjuntas and Utuado reporting unusual craft settling on surrounding mountains. Large spherical object observed disgorging smaller objects, all vanishing westward. Area highlighted for: copper mine test pits (Kennecott), speculated non-human interest in mineral deposits. Watchman Heriberto Acosta at Lajas municipal dump saw turtle-shell-shaped object whitish-lit underneath hovering ~60 ft above him, no lights but cast illumination like daylight, then suddenly ascended. Skywatch recordings (audio) by Willie Durand Urbina/PRRG document triangular objects departing Utuado toward Pellejas/Juan González/Jayuya, aircraft reconnoitering objects. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2015.
Locals near a limestone quarry saw strange lights and found metal pit covers torn open. The mayor wrote to President Bush requesting an investigation.

In broad daylight, a metallic disc silently hovered above a schoolyard before shooting upward and disappearing. Witnesses included schoolchildren and a teacher, whose accounts were consistent. The incident was reported in local media, increasing its credibility.

Several boys on crab-hunting expedition tried to capture a small green creature. Two boys described chasing it until it disappeared into a sewer hole; they could not follow. Creature described as having ugly red eyes, a pointed head like an ear of corn, equally pointed ears, and thin long arms. Boy Wilhelm Cordero stated it made sibilant noises and grabbed his arm before they fled. Other community residents had isolated encounters with the same tiny 'elf.' It also attempted contact with a child at the local kindergarten. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales, Earth's Shadow Denizens (2012).

While in Jardines de Adjuntas, Civic leader Noel González observed a large, silent object with a white beam of light moving overhead. Massive steel plates covering nearby test pits were later found torn apart, suggesting physical interaction.

location_name: Puerto Rico (island-wide, Puerto Rico Emergency Management Agency records)
description: Official internal memo from Col. José A.M. Nolla, Director of Puerto Rico State Civil Defense Agency (SAEA/PREMA), dated October 1991: confirmed Arecibo Radio Observatory staff were participating in a secret joint study. Multiple US military and security agencies along with Puerto Rican government agencies were investigating the high incidence of UFO and alien-related events affecting Puerto Rico in 1991-1992. Memo stated 'what was happening in Puerto Rico was real.' Source: Jorge Martín/IRAAP; government document.

Residents reported UFOs flying over community toward Barrio Guilarte. Noel González (civic leader) saw object of impressive proportions projecting white beam of light, moving slowly and noiselessly. Enormous steel plates covering Kennecott copper test pits allegedly torn open. Mayor Ramos wrote letter to President George W. Bush requesting investigation. Photos and video circulated.

location_name: Jardines de Adjuntas urbanization, Adjuntas, Puerto Rico
description: UFOs flying over Jardines de Adjuntas urbanization toward Barrio Guilarte. Respected civic leader Noel González reported an object of impressive proportions projecting a white beam of light, moving slowly and noiselessly. Steel plates covering Kennecott copper test pits allegedly torn asunder by unknown force (possibly beams from UFOs); photo/video evidence circulated. Mayor Rigoberto Ramos subsequently wrote to President George W. Bush requesting investigation. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2015.
Saucer-shaped craft chased by military jet observed by Freddie Cruz, then director of Civil Defense Agency of Lajas. Source: Alan Caviness research compilation.
Students & professors saw metallic disc hovering above campus. Photos allegedly taken but unreleased. Multi-witness academic sighting.

Several witnesses saw a large, round lighted craft descend behind a hill. A humanoid figure with glowing gray skin, long arms, large dark eyes and a column of light was observed 10 feet from the road. The being stood motionless as the observers fled the area. One witness also entered a trance-like state.
A jet aircraft, possibly an F-14, pursued a domed disc-shaped UFO. The object stopped mid-air and then split in half—one half flying southwest, the other east. The jet circled, then left the area.
Large luminous object projected light beam onto cattle research facility; witnessed by local police.

location_name: El Gigante hill, Adjuntas, Puerto Rico
description: Large luminous object descended on hill known as El Gigante near copper mine test pits. Police witnesses reported massive, noiseless object with lights underneath and single white beam illuminating hillside. Beam appeared to focus on agricultural school on El Gigante slopes. Reported by journalist Julio Victor Ramirez, radio personality Edwin Plaza.

location_name: El Gigante hill, Adjuntas (copper mine test pit sector), Puerto Rico
description: Early evening. Uncommonly large luminous object descended on El Gigante hill near copper mine test pits. Police officers present who told radio personality Edwin Plaza: object had lights underneath projecting a single white ray lighting up the hill, made no sound despite its massive size. Object's beam was apparently directed at an agricultural school on El Gigante slopes that focused on 'improved cattle ranching techniques.' Journalist Julio Víctor Ramírez covered the story for El Vocero. Source: Inexplicata/Scott Corrales 2015.

location_name: Las Minas Falls, El Yunque, Puerto Rico
description: Nelson Berríos and four friends picnicking near Las Minas Falls. Joaquín Ruiz photographed the area with 35mm Concord camera (400 ASA). Upon development, 4 sequential frames showed small humanoid creature with pale-pink skin and transparent punk-style standing hair, peering from behind a rock near the falls. Photographic evidence handed to Martín. Source: Jorge Martín investigation.
Pale‑yellow metallic object; cigar shape; silent hover 8–10s
Locals reported hovering craft emitting a low hum. Humanoids briefly visible near the craft. Possible geothermal/boron connection. Multiple corroborations.
Large blue light observed by Israel Acosta Meléndez and friends, hovered then accelerated away.

location_name: Cubuy sector, near El Yunque, Puerto Rico
description: 5:30 a.m. Victor Delgado driving to work in Carolina observed huge flying saucer hovering over slope near top of El Yunque mountain. Three tiers, many colored lights on underside circling, large yellow-green dome on top. Estimated 1,500 ft diameter. Same 'carrousel' type craft regularly observed by El Yunque area residents. Source: Jorge Martín.
Witnesses reported a humanoid figure near a landed disc.
Triangular UFO left fluorescent trail, seen by local family; moved silently at low altitude.
Shiny metallic stingray-shaped UFO seen by Aracelis Valentín; moved rapidly and silently.

location_name: Mount Briton watchtower, El Yunque, Puerto Rico
description: 10:30 p.m. Orlando Morales (WSKN news radio broadcaster, San Juan) and 4 friends camping at Mount Briton watchtower. Huge flying saucer (~600 ft diameter) descended from sky and overflew watchtower at low altitude emitting low hum. Craft descended into mist between mountains. One camper suffered nervous breakdown. Craft had classical saucer shape, many colored lights (blue, red, purple, yellow, orange), squared window openings ~5 ft each with bright white light. Surface metallic dull grey. US Forestry Service subsequently closed road 191 in El Yunque.

location_name: Río Blanco and Florida communities, Naguabo (near El Yunque), Puerto Rico
description: Multiple residents reported large UFOs similar to the Jan 14 Mount Briton craft flying into or emerging from El Yunque rain forest, over section of permanently closed road 191. Sightings lasted 30 minutes to 1 hour, day and night. US soldiers in black uniforms or camouflage without IDs confronted residents at gunpoint when they approached. Road 191 is not a military restricted zone. Source: Jorge Martín investigation.
1 witness found 8 sheep dead in March 1995, each with 3 puncture wounds and allegedly blood-loss claim – the first reported chupacabra attack . (No surviving human witness.) 1
1 eyewitness (Madelyne Tolentino) saw the chupacabra creature; soon after ~150 farm animals/pets were found killed on nearby farms . (Sighted by 1; animals dead.)

5-year-old child woke to noise, saw shadow running back and forth, then short dwarf-like figure outside room: thin, wide face, large pointed ears, huge dark eyes, light gray wrinkled skin. Figure made hand gestures, showed cross and piece of paper, then was retracted into metallic craft by light beam outside. Craft departed. Source: ThinkAboutItDocs.
Luminous disc ~40 ft diameter with row of dark windows positioned itself over antennae of Radio Procer radio station. Jorge Martín documented as part of 1994-1997 Chupacabras/UFO wave.

A 40-ft luminous disc with a rim of dark “windows” hovered over the station’s antennae. Electronic equipment malfunctioned, dials spun erratically, and a device from 1957 powered on despite being disconnected from electricity. Witnessed by radio staff.
Widespread livestock mutilations; ~1,000 animals reportedly blood-loss claim. Witnesses described a 4–6 ft tall creature with large oval eyes.
Nancy Martínez reported a manta-ray-like USO emerging from the sea near Playa Santa. Secondary-source intake; approximate date only.
A public skywatch was held at a known hotspot; while no UAPs appeared that night, the area is historically linked to high activity.
Expedition investigated alleged chupacabra nest after private briefing with mayor. Biological samples were collected for analysis.
Expedition revisited site of prior red-shift video anomaly; locals described time disturbances and a magnetic rock that interfered with instruments.
Woman claimed repeated contact with beings who abducted her unborn child; team mapped an octagonal magnetic field inside her home.
Researchers investigated local claims that luminous craft regularly entered and exited the lagoon through a submerged tunnel system.
Crop circle with uniformly bent grass discovered near the military aerostat; team reported intense dowsing activity and compass malfunctions.

During STS-80, shuttle video recorded a rapidly appearing luminous burst near Earth’s surface while the orbiter passed over the Caribbean region east of Puerto Rico. The object/event, labeled “F1” in Mark Carlotto’s analysis, appeared before orbital sunrise and outside major thunderstorm activity according to meteorological review in the paper. Frame analysis suggested extremely high apparent velocity and sudden luminosity. Carlotto argued the phenomenon did not neatly match known shuttle debris illumination effects or ordinary lightning signatures. The event was observed via shuttle imaging systems rather than direct human eyewitness testimony.

Puerto Rican UFO Flap of 1997 — numerous residents reported bright erratic lights and flying objects defying conventional flight patterns in Lajas and Cabo Rojo. Widely documented, became embedded in local culture. Lajas would later establish Ruta Extraterrestre (2005) due to decades-long sighting history.
Chupacabras attack killed 18 hens, 4 geese, 10 roosters. Police filed report; carcasses sent for analysis.
Students Torres & Quiñones saw a large UFO release 3 smaller craft near Aerostat. One entered sea, another toward Cabo Rojo. Multiple witnesses.

Witnesses and local officials reported a UFO “crash” near Laguna Cartagena. Officials claimed Coast Guard and mayor’s office involvement; multiple flames/fire reported. Footage and witness reports coordinated in UFO Casebook.

3:25 AM. Reports of UFO crash near town of Lajas. A bright object was witnessed by many people followed by a large crashing noise. Site of crash investigation near Laguna Cartagena area. Event took place near where Reinaldo Rios conducted monthly UFO observation camps. Source: UFO Casebook files.
Following an explosion, witnesses saw a glowing object crash into the hills. Primer Impacto aired footage; some claimed the military retrieved wreckage.
Video captured of silvery, rhomboidal object in the sky; reported on Univision’s Primer Impacto.
Driver encountered humanoid with glowing red eyes near cemetery.
Cross-shaped formation of lights moved slowly overhead.

location_name: Kianó Lagoon, western Vieques, Puerto Rico
description: Fisherman Luis Ortiz and others fishing at Kianó Lagoon observed small beings 3-3.5 ft tall, pale/white-gray skin, large bald heads, dark eyes, flattened faces, skinny chicken-like legs with 3-4 long toes, webbed hands, membrane between chest and upper arms. Beings moved at incredible speed, jumping and plunging into lagoon waters. Never showed aggression. Seen 'many times' over years. Lagoon sector later restricted by federal authorities citing contamination. Source: Jorge Martín, Vieques: Polígono del 3er. Tipo.
[Other] Laguna Carta
Multiple witnesses observed glowing object descend into ocean silently.

location_name: Sun Bay / La Mina area, Vieques, Puerto Rico
description: Luis Ortiz (resort employee) observed large oval object with bright yellow-orange light followed by 4 smaller objects (white/yellow/red/blue) flying east toward Camp García at ~9:00 PM. No engine or propeller sound. Objects stopped in midair. On one occasion two such objects passed toward east, then military jets followed, then explosions; only one object returned. Wife Mercedes Sanés observed from La Mina balcony: large golden-yellow light hovering 30+ minutes near Camp García before departing. Source: Jorge Martín, Vieques: Polígono del 3er. Tipo.
Silent, hovering disk above treeline; then shot away rapidly.
Witnesses saw a fireball descend; burnt vegetation & soil contamination found.
Fireball crashed in mountains, igniting grass fire; no wreckage found.
Antonio Sánchez family saw red fireball descend into yard. Witnesses ill with rashes, dizziness. EPA found NASA-patented oil.
Sphere observed near NASA rocket launch; behavior inconsistent with rocket.
Egg-shaped gray craft wobbling, then accelerated into clouds.
Three UFOs near moon observed after Hurricane Georges; disappeared suddenly.
Blue shining sphere rose from swampy area; flew deliberately, then vanished.
Cigar-shaped object stationary in sky, reflecting sunlight; then moved away.
Bright sphere flew low in daylight; silent; one photo taken.
Unspecified anomalous aerial phenomenon observed.
Mid-day sighting, shape not logged; possibly misfiled entry.
